quoteLast: ," he says, startled himself, and rather uncomfortable. "I do not understand why the messenger is not here with the conveyance.
title: Erlach Court
author: Schubin, Ossip, 1854-1934; Wister, A. L. (Annis Lee), 1830-1908 [Translator]

quoteFirst: Very proud we were as we ran into Albany at
quoteLast: on time to the minute, having made the run of one hundred and forty-two miles in one hundred and fifty-six minutes, an average of 53.8 miles an hour. This exceeds the average of the Chicago flyer, which is 48.
title: McClure's Magazine, Vol. 1, No. 4, September 1893

quoteLast: why they had not been summoned, for the Colonel had said in his curt epistle to Glyn--which "looked cross," so the boy said--that he would be at the Doctor's at eleven.

title: Jim Mortimer

quoteFirst: Will you have the kindness to lend me your carriage?" said Chichester elaborately. "Oh, don't talk! Get out quick. You can walk!" They changed horses quickly, and Chichester took the reins and drove on.
quoteLast: ; half past; quarter to twelve--and three miles yet to go! It was barely possible to do it. And perhaps it would have been done, if at that moment the good little Black Cock had not stumbled on a loose stone, gone down almost to his knees, and recovered himself with a violent wrench--lame! Chichester was a fair runner and a good walker.
title: Days Off, and Other Digressions
author: Van Dyke, Henry, 1852-1933

quoteFirst: It is considered that the bus lines are themselves a convenience to the public as their schedules and routes are planned to give service where the railroads do not. For example, a resident of Wahoo wishing to go to Omaha by train leaves at
quoteLast: , there being only one train a day, arrives at Omaha at 1:15 P.M.; but must wait until the next day to return, as the only train leaves Omaha at 12:41 P.M.; arriving at Wahoo at 2:31 P.M. The citizen has, therefore, spent practically two days to make the trip.
title: Highways and Highway Transportation
author: Chatburn, George R., 1863-1940

quoteFirst: “And, Markham, there are other reasons why we may eliminate strangers or casual prowlers. The person who sent Robin to his Maker must have been privy to the exact state of affairs in the Dillard house this morning between
quoteLast: and twenty minutes to twelve. He knew that Pyne and the old professor were alone there. He knew that Belle Dillard was not roaming about the premises.
title: The Bishop murder case
